articles.title: How a War Affects Prices in the UK
articles.summary: A war in the Gulf region is causing big problems for oil and gas prices around the world, including in the UK. The Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for shipping oil, is effectively closed because sailors are scared and insurance costs are high. This has made oil prices jump from $63 to $94 per barrel in just a few days. Gas prices in the UK have also gone up a lot, from 74 pence to £1.35 per therm. These higher energy costs mean things like fuel, food, and chemicals are getting more expensive, which is called inflation. The Bank of England might need to change interest rates to help control these rising prices. This situation reminds people of when energy prices went up during the Russia-Ukraine crisis, and it's making traders worried about the UK economy.

articles.title: Winter Paralympics: 6 Fun Sports for Athletes
articles.summary: The Winter Paralympics have started in Italy with a big opening ceremony. Over 600 athletes are competing for 79 medals in six exciting sports. These sports are Para alpine skiing, Para biathlon, Para cross-country skiing, Para ice hockey, Para snowboard, and wheelchair curling. In skiing sports, athletes can compete in three groups: standing, sitting on a special ski, or with vision impairment using a guide. They race in different events like slalom and downhill. The games began in 1976 and are held in cool places like Cortina. It's all about showing amazing skills and having fun in the snow!

articles.title: Gracie McGonigal: From Doubt to Bridgerton Star
articles.summary: Gracie McGonigal is an actor who was told she might not get many acting jobs because she has a disability—her arm stops below her elbow. But she didn't let that stop her. She loved acting since school and went to special arts schools to learn. Some people worried she wouldn't find roles, but Gracie kept trying. She even wore a fake hand in auditions at first, but a friend told her to be proud of her real arm. Now, she is in the popular Netflix show Bridgerton, proving that you can follow your dreams no matter what. Her story shows that being different can be a strength, and she encourages others to be themselves.

articles.title: U.S. Jobs and Economy Worries
articles.summary: In February, the U.S. economy lost 92,000 jobs, which is bad news for workers. The unemployment rate went up a little to 4.4%. This means fewer people have jobs, and it happened in many areas like health care and construction. Stocks, which are like pieces of companies people buy, fell down. Also, gas prices went up by 11% this week because of a war with Iran, making things more expensive. An expert named Diane Swonk explained that health care jobs dropped partly due to a strike, and even without that, job losses were high. Uncertainty from trade policies is hurting the economy too, acting like a tax. Overall, the economy is showing strain with job cuts and higher prices.

articles.title: U.S. to Refund Tariffs and Other News Stories
articles.summary: This article shares several news stories from a Friday. First, the U.S. government says it will start giving back money to companies in about 45 days for tariffs that were ruled illegal. Tariffs are taxes on goods from other countries. Second, big storms are moving across the central U.S., with warnings of possible tornadoes that could be dangerous. Third, a Texas congressman named Tony Gonzales says he will finish his job in Congress even though he won't run again after some personal issues. Fourth, the stock market had a bad week with prices dropping due to worries about war and oil. Lastly, people are remembering Bernard Lafayette, a civil rights activist who worked with Martin Luther King Jr. to promote nonviolence and voting rights.

articles.title: Americans Stuck Overseas During War
articles.summary: When a war started between the U.S. and Iran, the U.S. government told Americans to leave some countries quickly. But many airports closed, so thousands of Americans got stuck far from home. Over five days, about 24,000 people returned safely to the U.S. This article shares stories from Americans who were stranded. Emaan Abbass lived in Dubai and found it hard to leave. Colleen West was in Jerusalem with her husband and got back to Utah after the war began. Matt Gentile was in Iraq and returned to New York recently. John Almeida and his wife were in Dubai on vacation and got stuck just before their flight home. They all describe their struggles to return during the war.
